task_prompt: Based on the provided intelligence packet, predict the immediate 24-hour market reaction to this policy decision.

intelligence_packet.background: The target entity is the fourth-largest investment bank in the United States. The broader financial sector is currently experiencing a severe liquidity crisis driven by the collapse of the subprime mortgage market. Six months prior, the Federal Reserve intervened to rescue a rival bank (Bear Stearns) by financing its sale to JPMorgan Chase, establishing a precedent of government intervention for 'too big to fail' institutions.
intelligence_packet.catalyst_event: The Federal Reserve and U.S. Treasury officially announce they will not provide a financial guarantee to backstop the target entity's toxic assets, prompting Barclays and Bank of America to walk away from acquisition talks.
bullish_signals: Market participants widely assume the Federal Reserve is bluffing and will intervene at the 11th hour to prevent systemic contagion, citing the Bear Stearns precedent.
The firm's core prime brokerage and asset management divisions remain highly profitable and attractive to rival buyers if spun off.
A consortium of Wall Street banks has pledged a $70 billion borrowing facility to ensure general market liquidity, attempting to ring-fence the damage.
bearish_signals: Without a government guarantee, the firm has no acquirer and zero access to overnight repo funding, leaving it with virtually no operational liquidity.
The firm holds $85 billion in highly illiquid, heavily depreciated commercial real estate and subprime mortgage portfolios.
Counterparties and hedge fund clients are actively pulling their capital and demanding additional collateral, creating an uncontrollable bank run.
